perturbation.original_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising remuneration shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.050% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'advertising fees' to 'advertising remuneration' and altering the multiplier from 0.025% to 0.050%, introduces contradiction, because the 'advertising fees' term is the correct term used throughout the text, while 'advertising remuneration' creates confusion and changes the payment structure. This creates potential dispute about the correct calculation for payments. Also note that the new value is different from the original value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising remuneration shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.050% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'advertising fees' to 'advertising remuneration' and alters the percentage from 0.025% to 0.050%. The contradicted text uses both 'advertising fees' and 'advertising remuneration' and states that the percentage is 0.050%. The change introduces a contradiction because if the original agreement stated 0.025% and the contradicted section has 0.050%, they are contradictory. The use of both advertising terms also creates ambiguity, as the original and contradicted text appear in the same section. The shift in percentage creates a direct financial contradiction, and the interchangeable use of terms introduces ambiguity, satisfying the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction." The change makes enforcement of the payment terms unclear due to conflicting percentages.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be extended for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to extend this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another six (6) months.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'renewed' to 'extended' and changing 'one (1) year' to 'six (6) months' introduces an in-text contradiction. The section initially states the agreement automatically renews for 'one (1) year', but later states it renews for 'six (6) months' if no notice is given. It is unclear what the new renewal period is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be extended for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to extend this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another six (6) months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal period from 'one (1) year' to 'six (6) months' in the automatic renewal clause, while retaining the statement that the agreement can be 'extended for any number of successive one (1) year terms.' This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the renewed term if no notice is given, rendering the term ambiguous. The changed text and contradicted text are the same in this case.

perturbation.original_text: 9. INDEMNITY. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall indemnify the other party (the "Indemnified Party") against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which the Indemnified Party may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from the Indemnifying Party's acts, omissions or misrepresentations to the extent that the Indemnified Party is deemed a principal of the Indemnifying Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. INDEMNITY. Each side (the "Indemnifying Side") shall hold harmless the other side (the "Indemnified Side") against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which the Indemnified Side may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from the Indemnifying Side's acts, omissions or misrepresentations to the extent that the Indemnified Side is deemed an agent of the Indemnifying Side.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Indemnifying Party' to 'Indemnifying Side' and 'Indemnified Party' to 'Indemnified Side' , introduces a contradiction on the correct term being used, even thought the terms being used are still reffering to the same party/side. Also, changing from 'principal' to 'agent' introduces a direct contradiction related to the scope of the indemnity. A principal-agent relationship has very different legal implications than an indemnifying party relationship, creating uncertainly about the scope of the indemnity obligation. The term Party should be used to align with the rest of the text as this is the original wording.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. INDEMNITY. Each side (the "Indemnifying Side") shall hold harmless the other side (the "Indemnified Side") against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which the Indemnified Side may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from the Indemnifying Side's acts, omissions or misrepresentations to the extent that the Indemnified Side is deemed an agent of the Indemnifying Side. In addition, 2TheMart shall indemnify i-Escrow against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which i-Escrow may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from 2TheMart Content. In addition, i-Escrow shall indemnify 2TheMart against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which 2TheMart may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from i-Escrow Source: 2THEMART COM INC, 10-12G, 8/26/1999 Content and or the Services provided to Customers. The foregoing obligations are conditioned on the Indemnified Party: (i) giving the Indemnifying Party notice of the relevant claim, (ii) cooperating with the Indemnifying Party, at the Indemnifying Party's expense, in the defense of such claim, and (iii) giving the Indemnifying Party the right to control the defense and settlement of any such claim, except that the Indemnifying Party shall not enter into any settlement that affects the Indemnified Party's rights or interest without the Indemnified Party's prior written approval. The Indemnified Party shall have the right to participate in the defense at its expense.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Indemnifying Party' to 'Indemnifying Side' and 'Indemnified Party' to 'Indemnified Side', while seemingly minor, introduces a contradiction in terminology. The original text likely uses 'Party' consistently throughout the agreement. Changing this introduces ambiguity and inconsistency. More importantly, changing 'principal' to 'agent' fundamentally alters the legal relationship and the scope of indemnity, creating a direct contradiction. The indemnity obligations of a principal are different from those of an agent. This directly contradicts any section referring to the original intention of indemnity based on a principal-agent relationship.
